relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The journey tears away from beloved elders and moves island by island.
A ship toward Phoenicia carries the mission onward.
Cyprus falls to the side as Tyre receives the ship.
Disciples through the Spirit warn Paul about Jerusalem.
Whole households escort them to shore-prayer.
Farewell sends each back to their path.
Ptolemais offers one day with siblings.
Caesarea receives them in Philip the evangelist’s house.
Philip’s four daughters prophesy.
Agabus arrives from Judea.
The belt becomes a prophetic sign of binding and handing-over.
Love urges Paul away from Jerusalem.
Paul’s heart is ready for bonds and death for the name.
The community surrenders into the Lord’s will.
The ascent to Jerusalem begins.
An early disciple hosts the travelers.
Jerusalem siblings receive them gladly.
Paul meets James and the elders.
He tells the nations-story work by work.
They glorify the Divine and name Judean believers’ zeal for Law.
Rumor says Paul teaches abandonment of Moses among diaspora Judeans.
The question becomes how to answer the rumor.
Four vow-holders become the proposed sign.
Purification is offered to show Paul does not despise the Law.
The Gentile discernment from the council remains intact.
Paul enters the temple with the vow group.
Asia Judeans see him and stir the crowd.
The accusation is people, Law, place, and temple-commoning.
Assumption about Trophimus feeds the charge.
City movement becomes temple violence.
Death-intent reaches the Roman commander as report of confusion.
Soldiers interrupt the beating.
Paul is bound with chains as identity is questioned.
The uproar prevents certainty.
The crowd’s violence forces soldiers to carry him.
The shout is removal.
Paul speaks Greek to the commander.
The commander mistakes him for an Egyptian rebel.
Paul names his Judean and Tarsian identity and asks to speak.
On the stairs, he receives permission and addresses the people in Hebrew.
